component.name: TextArea
component.description: A multi-line text input for capturing longer free-form content such as comments, descriptions, messages, or notes. The multi-line sibling of TextField — it wraps a label, a bordered white container holding a native resizable <textarea>, and an optional helper message that can carry neutral, error, or success status.
component.note: Label is 12/16 semibold neutral.600; the textarea container is white with a 1px neutral.300 border and SHARP corners (radius-none, DS convention). The textarea fills it with 8px padding (6px vertical when compact), min-height 80px (64px compact), and is resizable vertically by default. Hover/focus are real CSS states (no `state` prop), matching Button/TextField. The helper message has three tones; error wins over success. Synced to Figma '❖ Text area'.

container.padding: spacing.base (8px) on the textarea; compact tightens vertical padding to spacing.xs (6px)

value.monospaced: isMonospaced renders the value in font.code (IBM Plex Mono)

spacing: spacing.xs (6px compact vertical padding / message icon↔text gap)
tokens.focusRing: 2px colors.border.primary.strong (forest.700), offset 1px, on the container; switches to danger.700 when invalid. The inner textarea has outline:none.

useCases: Capture multi-line free-form text — comments, descriptions, messages, notes
A long-form field inside a form section with a label, helper text, and validation
A bio or about field where the user may write several lines or paragraphs
Code or config entry via isMonospaced (renders the value in IBM Plex Mono)
Feedback or message-box input where line wrapping and vertical resize help

commonPatterns.name: Labeled multi-line field
commonPatterns.description: Standard textarea with a label and placeholder inside a form.
commonPatterns.jsx: <TextArea label='Description' placeholder='Tell us more…' value={text} onChange={(e) => setText(e.target.value)} />

commonPatterns.name: Required field with validation
commonPatterns.description: Required textarea that surfaces an error message + warning icon when invalid.
commonPatterns.jsx: <TextArea label='Message' isRequired isInvalid={!!error} helperText={error ?? 'Up to 500 characters.'} maxLength={500} />

commonPatterns.name: Success confirmation
commonPatterns.description: Valid tone shows a forest message with a check-circle icon after a passing check.

commonPatterns.name: Code input
commonPatterns.description: Monospaced textarea with no resize, used for a config or snippet field.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using TextArea for a single short value
antiPatterns.reason: A one-line value does not need wrapping, multiple rows, or resize, and reads as too large.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use TextField for names, emails, search, and other single-line entry.

antiPatterns.reason: The textarea has no accessible name and is unusable by screen readers.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass label, or set aria-label for a standalone textarea.

antiPatterns.scenario: Conveying the error only through the red border
antiPatterns.reason: Color alone fails accessibility; users who can't perceive the color get no error.
antiPatterns.alternative: Pass helperText with isInvalid so the message text, warning icon, and aria role=alert all carry the error.

antiPatterns.scenario: Disabling resize without a good reason
antiPatterns.reason: Vertical resize lets users see more of their content; removing it hurts long-form entry.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep the default vertical resize; only set resize='none' for fixed layouts like code blocks.

antiPatterns.reason: The code font is for code/config; it makes ordinary sentences harder to read.
antiPatterns.alternative: Leave the default font for prose; reserve isMonospaced for code or structured text.

slots.label: Optional — text above the textarea (omit only for standalone fields, then set aria-label)
slots.helperText: Optional helper/status message below the textarea (the Figma <Message>)

interactions.hover: border darkens to neutral.400 (only when not disabled and not focused)
interactions.focus: focus-within → forest.500 border + 2px forest.700 outline offset 1px on the container; inner textarea has outline:none
interactions.filled: typed value renders in neutral.800 at 14/20; placeholder is neutral.500
interactions.invalid: danger.500 border in every state; focus ring switches to danger.700; sets aria-invalid; pair with an error helperText
interactions.valid: success tone on the helper message (forest.500 text + check-circle icon); border is unchanged
interactions.disabled: neutral.50 fill, neutral.300 border, neutral.400 text, cursor not-allowed, resize forced to none

behavior.resize: Resizable vertically by default; the `resize` prop accepts vertical | horizontal | both | none. Disabled forces resize to none. The textarea remains keyboard-resizable.
behavior.toneResolution: Helper tone is error if isInvalid, else success if isValid, else neutral — error always wins over success.
behavior.controlled: Pass value + onChange for controlled; omit value and pass defaultValue for uncontrolled. ref and remaining textarea attributes (rows defaults to 4, maxLength, name, etc.) are forwarded to the native <textarea>.

accessibility.focusRing: 2px forest.700 outline, offset 1px, on the container (danger.700 when invalid)
accessibility.labelWiring: label is linked to the textarea via htmlFor/id; a standalone field (no label) must carry aria-label
accessibility.requiredState: isRequired sets the native required attribute (and its implicit aria) plus a danger asterisk on the label
accessibility.describedBy: helperText is linked to the textarea through aria-describedby; the error message has role=alert
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab moves focus in/out; standard text-editing keys apply; the textarea stays keyboard-resizable
accessibility.screenReader: Native textarea drives the accessible name from the associated label or aria-label; error messages are announced via role=alert
accessibility.contrastNote: Error MESSAGE text uses danger.700 (5.81:1 on white) so 12px text passes AA; the danger.500 border is a 3:1 non-text element and passes.

athenaConstraints.errorMessageColor: danger.700 (--text-danger-strong) — never danger.500 for the message text

figmaDivergences.figma: explicit state property (default / hover / focus / typing / filled)
figmaDivergences.implementation: real CSS states (:hover, :focus-within) plus the value the user types
figmaDivergences.reason: Matches Button/TextField; states are driven by the browser and the textarea value rather than a prop.
danger.500 is only 3.54:1 on white and fails AA for 12px text; danger.700 is 5.81:1 and passes. The danger BORDER stays danger.500 (a 3:1 non-text element, which passes).

figmaDivergences.figma: a Figma-only layer that fakes the textarea's min-height
figmaDivergences.implementation: a real CSS min-height (80px / 64px compact) plus the `rows` attribute (defaults to 4)
figmaDivergences.reason: The Figma hack has no runtime equivalent; min-height and rows produce the actual minimum size in the browser.

aiHints.context: Reach for TextArea for multi-line / long-form text such as comments, descriptions, messages, or notes; reach for TextField for single-line input. Always give it a label (or aria-label). Use isRequired for required fields, isInvalid + an error helperText to surface validation, isValid for success confirmation, isCompact for dense layouts, and isMonospaced for code or config. The default vertical resize suits long-form entry; set resize='none' only for fixed layouts.
neverUseWhen: The value is a single short line — use TextField
There is no label and no aria-label — the field has no accessible name
An error is shown only via the border color — pair isInvalid with an error helperText
You disable resize without a layout reason — keep the default vertical resize
You apply isMonospaced to prose — reserve the code font for code or structured text
